briefdescription.para: The operation returned successfully.
There was not enough backing storage to complete the operation.
One or more arguments were invalid or uninitialized.
The lengths and/or count of the dictionary its tuples are inconsistent.
A requested operation required additional memory to be allocated, but the allocation failed, likely due to insufficient remaining heap memory.
Return values for dictionary write/conversion functions.
The value is an array of bytes.
The value is a zero-terminated, UTF-8 C-string.
The value is an unsigned integer. The tuple's .length field is used to determine the size of the integer (1, 2, or 4 bytes).
The value is a signed integer. The tuple's .length field is used to determine the size of the integer (1, 2, or 4 bytes).
Values representing the type of data that the value field of a Tuple contains.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* DictionaryKeyUpdatedCallback) (const uint32_t key, const Tuple *new_tuple, const Tuple *old_tuple, void *context)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const uint32_t key, const Tuple *new_tuple, const Tuple *old_tuple, void *context)
memberdef.name: DictionaryKeyUpdatedCallback

briefdescription.para: Type of the callback used in dict_merge()

parameterdescription.para: The key that is being updated.

The new tuple. The tuple points to the actual, updated destination dictionary or NULL_TUPLE in case there was an error (e.g. backing buffer was too small). Therefore the Tuple can be used after the callback returns, until the destination dictionary storage is free'd (by the application itself).

The values that will be replaced with new_tuple. The key, value and type will be equal to the previous tuple in the old destination dictionary, however the old_tuple points to a stack-allocated copy of the old data. @param context Pointer to application specific data The storage backing old_tuple` can only be used during the callback and will no longer be valid after the callback returns.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* DictionarySerializeCallback) (const uint8_t *const data, const uint16_t size, void *context)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const uint8_t *const data, const uint16_t size, void *context)
memberdef.name: DictionarySerializeCallback

briefdescription.para: Callback for dict_serialize_tuplets() utility.

parameterdescription.para: The data of the serialized dictionary
The context pointer as passed in to dict_serialize_tuplets()

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint8_t tuple_count,...)
memberdef.name: dict_calc_buffer_size

briefdescription.para: Calculates the number of bytes that a dictionary will occupy, given one or more value lengths that need to be stored in the dictionary.

simplesect.para: The formula to calculate the size of a Dictionary in bytes is: 1 + (n * 7) + D1 + ... + Dn Where n is the number of Tuples in the Dictionary and Dx are the sizes of the values in the Tuples. The size of the Dictionary header is 1 byte. The size of the header for each Tuple is 7 bytes.

parameterdescription.para: The total number of key/value pairs in the dictionary.
The sizes of each of the values that need to be stored in the dictionary.

simplesect.para: The total number of bytes of storage needed.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Tuplet *const tuplets, const uint8_t tuplets_count)
memberdef.name: dict_calc_buffer_size_from_tuplets

briefdescription.para: Calculates the number of bytes that a dictionary will occupy, given one or more Tuplets that need to be stored in the dictionary.

simplesect.para: See dict_calc_buffer_size() for the formula for the calculation.

parameterdescription.para: An array of Tuplets that need to be stored in the dictionary.
The total number of Tuplets that follow.

simplesect.para: The total number of bytes of storage needed.

memberdef.argsstring: (const DictionaryIterator *iter, const uint32_t key)
memberdef.name: dict_find

briefdescription.para: Tries to find a Tuple with specified key in a dictionary.

parameterdescription.para: Iterator to the dictionary to search in.
The key for which to find a Tuple

simplesect.para: Pointer to a found Tuple, or NULL if there was no Tuple with the specified key.

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *dest, uint32_t *dest_max_size_in_out, DictionaryIterator *source, const bool update_existing_keys_only, const DictionaryKeyUpdatedCallback key_callback, void *context)
memberdef.name: dict_merge

briefdescription.para: Merges entries from another "source" dictionary into a "destination" dictionary. All Tuples from the source are written into the destination dictionary, while updating the exsting Tuples with matching keys.

parameterdescription.para: In: the maximum size of buffer backing dest. Out: the final size of the updated dictionary.
The source dictionary of which its Tuples will be used to update dest.
Specify True if only the existing keys in dest should be updated.
The callback that will be called for each Tuple in the merged destination dictionary.
Pointer to app specific data that will get passed in when update_key_callback is called.

simplesect.para: DICT_OK, DICT_INVALID_ARGS, DICT_NOT_ENOUGH_STORAGE

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const uint8_t *const buffer, const uint16_t size)
memberdef.name: dict_read_begin_from_buffer

briefdescription.para: Initializes the dictionary iterator with a given buffer and size, in preparation of reading key/value tuples.

parameterdescription.para: The storage size of the dictionary

simplesect.para: The first tuple in the dictionary, or NULL in case the dictionary was empty or if there was a parsing error.

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter)
memberdef.name: dict_read_first

briefdescription.para: Resets the iterator back to the same state as a call to dict_read_begin_from_buffer() would do.

simplesect.para: The first tuple in the dictionary, or NULL in case the dictionary was empty or if there was a parsing error.

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter)
memberdef.name: dict_read_next

briefdescription.para: Progresses the iterator to the next key/value pair.

simplesect.para: The next tuple in the dictionary, or NULL in case the end has been reached or if there was a parsing error.

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionarySerializeCallback callback, void *context, const Tuplet *const tuplets, const uint8_t tuplets_count)
memberdef.name: dict_serialize_tuplets

briefdescription.para: Utility function that takes a list of Tuplets from which a dictionary will be serialized, ready to transmit or store.

simplesect.para: The callback will be called before the function returns, so the data that that context points to, can be stack allocated.

parameterdescription.para: The callback that will be called with the serialized data of the generated dictionary.
Pointer to any application specific data that gets passed into the callback.
An array of Tuplets that need to be serialized into the dictionary.
The number of tuplets that follow.

simplesect.para: DICT_OK, DICT_NOT_ENOUGH_STORAGE or DICT_INVALID_ARGS

memberdef.argsstring: (const Tuplet *const tuplets, const uint8_t tuplets_count, uint8_t *buffer, uint32_t *size_in_out)
memberdef.name: dict_serialize_tuplets_to_buffer

briefdescription.para: Utility function that takes an array of Tuplets and serializes them into a dictionary with a given buffer and size.

parameterdescription.para: The number of tuplets in the array
The buffer in which to write the serialized dictionary
The available buffer size in bytes

simplesect.para: DICT_OK, DICT_NOT_ENOUGH_STORAGE or DICT_INVALID_ARGS

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const Tuplet *const tuplets, const uint8_t tuplets_count, uint8_t *buffer, uint32_t *size_in_out)
memberdef.name: dict_serialize_tuplets_to_buffer_with_iter

briefdescription.para: Serializes an array of Tuplets into a dictionary with a given buffer and size.

parameterdescription.para: The number of tuplets in the array
The buffer in which to write the serialized dictionary
The available buffer size in bytes

simplesect.para: DICT_OK, DICT_NOT_ENOUGH_STORAGE or DICT_INVALID_ARGS

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter)
memberdef.name: dict_size

briefdescription.para: Calculates the size of data that has been written to the dictionary. AKA, the "dictionary size". Note that this is most likely different than the size of the backing storage/backing buffer.

simplesect.para: The total number of bytes which have been written to the dictionary.

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, uint8_t *const buffer, const uint16_t size)
memberdef.name: dict_write_begin

briefdescription.para: Initializes the dictionary iterator with a given buffer and size, resets and empties it, in preparation of writing key/value tuples.

parameterdescription.para: The storage size of the dictionary

simplesect.para: DICT_OK, DICT_NOT_ENOUGH_STORAGE or DICT_INVALID_ARGS

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const uint32_t key, const char *const cstring)
memberdef.name: dict_write_cstring

briefdescription.para: Adds a key with a C string value pair to the dictionary.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the zero-terminated C string

simplesect.para: DICT_OK, DICT_NOT_ENOUGH_STORAGE or DICT_INVALID_ARGS
The string will be copied into the backing storage of the dictionary.
There is no checking for duplicate keys.

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const uint32_t key, const uint8_t *const data, const uint16_t size)
memberdef.name: dict_write_data

briefdescription.para: Adds a key with a byte array value pair to the dictionary.

simplesect.para: DICT_OK, DICT_NOT_ENOUGH_STORAGE or DICT_INVALID_ARGS
The data will be copied into the backing storage of the dictionary.
There is no checking for duplicate keys.

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter)
memberdef.name: dict_write_end

briefdescription.para: End a series of writing operations to a dictionary. This must be called before reading back from the dictionary.

simplesect.para: The size in bytes of the finalized dictionary, or 0 if the parameters were invalid.

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const uint32_t key, const void *integer, const uint8_t width_bytes, const bool is_signed)
memberdef.name: dict_write_int

briefdescription.para: Adds a key with an integer value pair to the dictionary.

parameterdescription.para: The width of the integer value
Whether the integer's type is signed or not

simplesect.para: DICT_OK, DICT_NOT_ENOUGH_STORAGE or DICT_INVALID_ARGS
There is no checking for duplicate keys. dict_write_int() is only for serializing a single integer. width_bytes can only be 1, 2, or 4.

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const uint32_t key, const int16_t value)
memberdef.name: dict_write_int16

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const uint32_t key, const int32_t value)
memberdef.name: dict_write_int32

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const uint32_t key, const int8_t value)
memberdef.name: dict_write_int8

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const Tuplet *const tuplet)
memberdef.name: dict_write_tuplet

briefdescription.para: Serializes a Tuplet and writes the resulting Tuple into a dictionary.

parameterdescription.para: The Tuplet describing the key/value pair to write

simplesect.para: DICT_OK, DICT_NOT_ENOUGH_STORAGE or DICT_INVALID_ARGS

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const uint32_t key, const uint16_t value)
memberdef.name: dict_write_uint16

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const uint32_t key, const uint32_t value)
memberdef.name: dict_write_uint32

memberdef.argsstring: (DictionaryIterator *iter, const uint32_t key, const uint8_t value)
memberdef.name: dict_write_uint8

briefdescription.para: Adds a key with an unsigned, 8-bit integer value pair to the dictionary.

simplesect.para: DICT_OK, DICT_NOT_ENOUGH_STORAGE or DICT_INVALID_ARGS
There is no checking for duplicate keys.

There are counterpart functions for different signedness and widths, dict_write_uint16(), dict_write_uint32(), dict_write_int8(), dict_write_int16() and dict_write_int32(). The documentation is not repeated for brevity's sake.

briefdescription.para: Macro to create a Tuplet with a byte array value.
Macro to create a Tuplet with a c-string value.
Macro to create a Tuplet with an integer value.
Data serialization utilities

detaileddescription.para: Data residing in different parts of Pebble memory (RAM) may need to be gathered and assembled into a single continuous block for transport over the network via Bluetooth. The process of gathering and assembling this continuous block of data is called serialization.

You use data serialization utilities, like Dictionary, Tuple and Tuplet data structures and accompanying functions, to accomplish this task. No transformations are performed on the actual data, however. These Pebble utilities simply help assemble the data into one continuous buffer according to a specific format.
AppMessage uses these utilitiesin particular, Dictionaryto send information between mobile and Pebble watchapps.
To iterate over the key/value pairs in the dictionary that was created in the previous example code, you would do this:

To understand the difference between Tuple and Tuplet data structures: Tuple is the header for a serialized key/value pair, while Tuplet is a helper data structure that references the value you want to serialize. This data structure exists to make the creation of a Dictionary easier to write. Use this mnemonic to remember the difference: TupleT(emplate), the Tuplet being a template to create a Dictionary with Tuple structures.
